tweets.text: The U.S. trade deficit soared by 42% in May to $77.6B, as imports rose and exports fell. For all Trump's rhetoric and tariffs meant to reduce our trade deficits, we are more reliant than ever on the rest of the world to produce what we consume and lend us the money to buy it.

tweets.text: On CNBC @steveliesman pointed out that people want health care. Of course they do. People want lots of stuff. But that doesn't mean it's the government's job to provide it. The government's only legitimate role is to stay out of the way so people can pursue that stuff themselves.

thread_tweets.text:
after thoughts, wow there is intense emotional debate, especially around the var, was at one point $65k deep in this trade, sold off a lot at 2.5 and 1.1 odds when it was egypt winning, but also kept a decent chunk for fun

my take on the var is its just obviously a foul, technically, but you have to leaning more neutral than a 100% foul because of how foul works in the first place, not every foul is called this wc, actually tons of defending teams tend to not get fouls calledin those areas of the pitch this world cup, if u watch ull understand immediately, but since it led to a goal it had to be checked, 

i think it just really made ppl hate var this game was that egypt went on to score what imo was 1 of the best counter attack goal of the world cup precise passing, speed, dribbling, perfect shot on goal, they made it too pretty to be disallowed so now all the fans hate it but it is what it is

also that lautaro martinez cross, a lot of people crossing the ball is just a rough area and pray, he only had 1 option in the box, and it couldnt been more perfect, tbh top 1 cross for sure this wc for me, i cant possibly see how this one will be topped
